---
title: "Studies"
lang: en
language: ../../assets/language-en.yml
toc-title: "On this page"
---

This page lists currently summarized BMAA-related studies from newest to oldest.

| Year | Study |
|---|---|
| 2026 | [From body to mind: How body-mind axial awareness training enhances body awareness, mindfulness, positive embodiment and mental health in healthcare students](2026-tien-healthcare-students.qmd) |
| 2026 | [From the body to the mind: Interoception and sense of agency as mechanisms of depression reduction in BMAA](2026-lien-depression-agency.qmd) |
| 2024 | [Effects of BMAA combined with lucid-dream therapy on lucid dreaming, nightmares, and sleep quality among people with nightmare distress](2024-chiu-nightmare-lucid-dream.qmd) |
| 2024 | [Improving interoceptive ability to reduce internet addiction tendency: Effects and mechanisms of BMAA training](2024-mou-internet-addiction.qmd) |
| 2024 | [Effects of BMAA on interoception, alexithymia, and empathy among adults with high autistic traits](2024-yang-autistic-traits.qmd) |
| 2023 | [A preliminary study of a camp-based dynamic contemplative course on children’s sustained attention and balance](2023-yeh-camp-attention-balance.qmd) |
| 2023 | [Brief mindfulness guidance reduces implicit gender stereotypes during reading](2023-hung-gender-stereotype.qmd) |
| 2020 | [Effects of a short-term dynamic body-mind awareness course on elementary school teachers’ interoceptive awareness, resilience, and perceived stress](2020-weng-teachers-stress-resilience.qmd) |
| 2020 | [Effects of a two-week BMAA practice on emotion regulation among university students](2020-hsu-emotion-regulation.qmd) |
| 2019 | [Feel your body: Effects of a short-term in-class dynamic contemplative course on children’s interoception, executive control, emotion regulation, and motor coordination](2019-lee-feel-your-body.qmd) |
| 2018 | [BMAA and integrated activity training for improving children’s executive function and emotion regulation](2018-chang-children-executive-emotion.qmd) |
| 2017 | [An initial exploration of body-mind interaction: Effects of BMAA on body sensation, working memory, and attentional control](2017-lee-body-mind-interaction.qmd) |
| 2016 | [What Confucius practiced is good for your mind: Examining the effect of a contemplative practice in Confucian tradition on executive functions](2016-teng-confucius.qmd) |

## References

Tien, Y.-M., Lin, C.-Y., Lien, Y.-W., & Hsu, L.-C. (2026). From body to mind: How body-mind axial awareness training enhances body awareness, mindfulness, positive embodiment and mental health in healthcare students. *Health Psychology and Behavioral Medicine, 14*(1), Article 2656029. https://doi.org/10.1080/21642850.2026.2656029

Lien, Y.-W., Teng, S.-C., & Wei, L.-Y. (2026). From the body to the mind: Interoception and sense of agency as mechanisms of depression reduction in the Body-Mind Axial Awareness (BMAA). *Frontiers in Psychology, 17*, Article 1755698. https://doi.org/10.3389/fpsyg.2026.1755698

邱敬惠. (2024). *身心中軸覺察訓練結合清醒夢療法對惡夢困擾者的清醒夢、惡夢與睡眠品質的影響* [碩士論文，中原大學].

牟書琪. (2024). *內感覺能力提升對於降低網路成癮傾向的影響：以身心中軸覺察訓練為介入的效果與機制探討* [碩士論文，中原大學].

楊捷如. (2024). *身心中軸覺察對於高自閉症特質成年人之內感覺、述情障礙及同理心的影響* [碩士論文，中原大學].

葉淨維, 連韻文, 陳人瑜, 莊博雅, 鄭惟馨, & 陳顥齡. (2023). 營隊式動態靜觀課程對兒童持續注意力與平衡能力影響之初探. *台灣職能治療研究與實務, 19*(1), 81-97.

洪珮瑩, 陳苡禕, & 葉理豪. (2023). 短期正念引導降低閱讀時的內隱性別刻板印象. *教育心理學報, 54*(3), 515-536. https://doi.org/10.6251/BEP.202303_54(3).0001

翁宛婷. (2020). *探討短期動態身心覺察課程對國小教師內感覺覺察、心理韌性與壓力知覺的介入成效* [碩士論文，國立臺灣師範大學].

許惟智. (2020). *探討兩週身心中軸覺察練習對大學生情緒調節的成效及覺察傾向、工作記憶的中介角色* [碩士論文，國立臺灣大學]. https://doi.org/10.6342/NTU202002076

李茂寧. (2019). *感覺你的身體：短期入班動態靜觀課程對學童內感覺、執行控制功能、情緒調節及運動協調之介入成效* [碩士論文，國立臺灣大學].

張憶如. (2018). *身心中軸覺察與綜合活動訓練增進兒童之執行功能及情緒調節* [碩士論文，中原大學].

李少揚. (2017). *身心互動途徑初探：以「身心中軸覺察訓練」對身體感覺、工作記憶與注意力控制功能的提升效果為例* [碩士論文，國立臺灣大學]. https://doi.org/10.6342/NTU201700254

Teng, S.-C., & Lien, Y.-W. (2016). What Confucius practiced is good for your mind: Examining the effect of a contemplative practice in Confucian tradition on executive functions. *Consciousness and Cognition, 42*, 204-215. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.concog.2016.03.016
